title: docs: Error in Example Requirer
summary: The integrate how-to (docs/how-to/integrate.md) shows an IngressPerAppRequirer example that observes self.ingress.ready / self.ingress.revoked. Those attributes don't exist on the requirer object (events live on .on), so copying the example raises AttributeError: 'IngressPerAppRequirer' object has no attribute 'ready'. Should be self.ingress.on.ready / self.ingress.on.revoked.
why_good_first_issue: Two-line, mechanical docs fix with an unambiguous correct form. The library's own docstring already uses the correct .on.ready form (lib/charms/traefik_k8s/v2/ingress.py:41,44), so the fix is verifiable against in-repo code. No runtime needed.
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc "cd ~/work/traefik-k8s-operator && sed -n '55,70p' docs/how-to/integrate.md" ; then compare with lib docstring: grep -n 'self.ingress.on.ready' lib/charms/traefik_k8s/v2/ingress.py
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection: docs/how-to/integrate.md line 62 `self.framework.observe(self.ingress.ready, self._on_ingress_ready)` and line 63 `self.framework.observe(self.ingress.revoked, self._on_ingress_revoked)`. The requirer exposes events via .on (IngressPerAppRequirerEvents), so self.ingress.ready does not exist -> AttributeError at observe() time. Expected (and used in the same lib's own docstring at ingress.py:41/44): self.ingress.on.ready / self.ingress.on.revoked. Observed-vs-expected mismatch is in-repo and exact.
fix_hint: In docs/how-to/integrate.md change `self.ingress.ready` -> `self.ingress.on.ready` and `self.ingress.revoked` -> `self.ingress.on.revoked` (lines 62-63). Bonus: line 57 imports IngressReadyEvent but the handler annotation on line 64 uses IngressPerAppReadyEvent; align the import/annotation while there.
notes: Reported from documentation.ubuntu.com/traefik-k8s-charm how-to/integrate; the same buggy snippet is checked into the repo docs, so it is fixable in-tree (not only on the external site). Charmhub rendered page already correct.

title: [charm-lib] Inconsistent and missing docstrings for constructor parameters in v2.ingress
summary: IngressPerAppRequirer.__init__ in lib/charms/traefik_k8s/v2/ingress.py accepts port, strip_prefix, redirect_https, scheme, etc., but the Args: docstring is incomplete/inconsistent: `port` is not listed in Args: at all (only cryptically under a trailing 'Request Args:'), and strip_prefix/redirect_https are documented with one-liners that don't explain routing-mode interaction or defaults. Charm authors must read the implementation to use the lib.
why_good_first_issue: Pure docstring editing in one well-bounded constructor; no behaviour change. The gaps are concrete and enumerable (port missing from Args, strip_prefix under-explained). Done = every accepted parameter has type + description + default in the Args section.
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc "cd ~/work/traefik-k8s-operator && sed -n '704,760p' lib/charms/traefik_k8s/v2/ingress.py"
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection: signature (lines 704-718) declares host, ip, port, strip_prefix, redirect_https, scheme, healthcheck_params. Args: section (lines 727-757) documents charm, relation_name, host, ip, healthcheck_params, strip_prefix, redirect_https, scheme -- but `port` is ABSENT from Args: (only appears under a separate trailing 'Request Args:' at lines 756-757 as 'port: the port of the service'). strip_prefix (line 751) reads only 'Configure Traefik to strip the path prefix.' with no routing-mode/default detail, exactly as the issue states. Observed: inconsistent/incomplete constructor docs; Expected: every param documented with type/description/default.
fix_hint: Complete and normalise the Args: block: add `port` (with default None and the 'at least port is required if any request arg set' caveat), expand strip_prefix (interaction with path/subdomain routing modes; default False), expand redirect_https (which leg of TLS it affects; default False). Bump LIBPATCH since the lib changed.
notes: Issue also mentions the --trust/403 error-state behaviour being undocumented (overlaps with #589); scope the good-first-issue to the docstring completeness only.
